text.title: AIOps Core Capabilities, Architecture, and Tool Landscape
text.summary: Five AIOps capabilities, data-flow architecture, tool comparison, 2025-2026 evolution trends, and success metrics with targets.

list.item: Anomaly detection: identify deviations from normal using ML (Isolation Forest, Prophet, LSTM)
Root cause analysis: determine failure origin via event correlation, causal graphs, topology analysis
Predictive alerts: forecast failures before impact using time-series forecasting
Auto-remediation: self-healing actions (restart, scale, rollback) with risk-based approval gates
Capacity planning: resource demand forecasting using regression and seasonal decomposition

section.title: Data-Flow Architecture
section.p: AIOps requires unified telemetry collection before any ML or LLM analysis can be applied.

list.item: Data sources: metrics (Prometheus), logs (Fluent Bit), traces (Jaeger/OpenTelemetry), events (K8s), changes (Git/CD)
Ingestion: OpenTelemetry Collector normalizes across sources into unified format
Analysis: ML models for anomaly detection + LLM for incident summaries and runbook generation
Action: auto-remediation scripts + human-approval gates + SOAR/ChatOps integration
LLM integration: natural language incident summaries, GenAI runbooks generated from topology + history
Predictive operations: failures predicted and prevented before reaching production
Hybrid workflows: AI recommends → humans approve → systems execute (not fully autonomous)
Explainable AI: every auto-action logged with reasoning for audit trail
SLO-aware detection: alerts based on error budget burn rate, not static thresholds
Change-aware correlation: RCA considers recent deployments and config changes in root-cause ranking

rule.statement: Classify every auto-remediation action by risk level and require human approval for P1/P2 incidents.
rule.rationale: Auto-remediation without approval gates can cascade failures. A pod restart in response to an anomaly might be correct 95% of the time, but the 5% case could be a symptom of a wider outage requiring human coordination.

list.item: Low risk (auto-execute): pod restart on CrashLoopBackOff, HPA scale on CPU above 80%, cache flush on memory pressure
Medium risk (notify + short timeout): node drain on unhealthy node, deployment rollback on error spike post-deploy
High risk (require human approval): cluster-wide operations, cross-service changes, data-touching actions
Anomaly precision: target above 60% after tuning (true incidents / total anomalies promoted)
Auto-remediation rate: start above 15%, grow to above 40% over 6 months
MTTR reduction: target above 50% improvement vs pre-AIOps baseline
False positive rate: target below 20%
Noise reduction: target above 80% (correlated alerts / raw alert count)
Native OpenTelemetry support (unified metrics, logs, traces)
SLO-aware detection with error-budget burn-rate alerts
Change-aware correlation (deployment and config change events in RCA)
Service topology with automatic dependency discovery
LLM/RAG integration for intelligent runbook generation
